title: How Retrieval-Augmented Generation Works
decision: understand when retrieval improves an AI product and when it does not
prompt:
Create a seven-slide educational explainer for non-specialist product managers learning when retrieval-augmented generation helps. Progress from the failure of memory-only answers through indexing, retrieval, grounded generation, evaluation, limitations, and a practical decision checklist. Include a progressive editable diagram, a simple timeline of one query, an analogy, and a final summary. Keep technical terms defined in plain language.

Design direction: Use an evidence-led editorial direction: restrained surface treatment, decisive hierarchy, visible provenance, and an inspectable visual argument on every proof slide. Avoid card grids and decorative dashboards.

attachments.content: The benchmark definition is: index approved documents, retrieve passages relevant to a query, provide those passages to the generator, and evaluate whether claims are supported by the retrieved text. Retrieval does not guarantee truth and can return irrelevant evidence.

designDirection.label: Evidence editorial
designDirection.instruction: Use an evidence-led editorial direction: restrained surface treatment, decisive hierarchy, visible provenance, and an inspectable visual argument on every proof slide. Avoid card grids and decorative dashboards.

trace.summary: The user consented to send the full brief and uploaded data sources to OpenRouter. NodeSlide used its deterministic fallback because The Gemini 3.5 Flash via OpenRouter route had no compatible provider endpoint. Self-critique: 1 pass (deterministic route; self-critique loop skipped).
